description:
Roll-up epic for the four bugs and one task surfaced by the cross-project
mutagen efficacy study (2026-05-18). All four child tickets are independent
and can be implemented in any order; orchestrate-epic should pick them up
by priority (1jx is P1, the others P2/P3).

## Background

The v1 efficacy study (2026-05-17) dogfooded mutagen on mutagen_ex's own
tests and surfaced the wrd.40 archive-install bug as a side effect. The v2
cross-project study (2026-05-18) ran the same playbook against three more
projects:
- **atlas** (Phoenix/Ecto knowledge graph) — 13 runs, 153 sites, kill rate 0.67
- **builder** (Phoenix LiveView admin) — 8 runs, 218 sites, kill rate 0.70
- **engage** — study-not-feasible (thin corpus + DB coupling)

Aggregate kill rates converged in a tight 0.67-0.75 band across all three
working projects — mutagen's signal is robust. But four mutagen-side
failure modes surfaced consistently enough to be worth fixing as a single
adoption-polish epic:

## Children

- **mutagen-1jx** (P1, bug) — Runtime preamble: start host OTP application
  before tests in archive-install mode. **Two independent reproductions
  (atlas + builder)**, same shape as wrd.40 archive-install fix. Atlas
  worked around with a 12-line `Mix.Tasks.MutagenApp` shim that's a usable
  reference implementation. Highest-leverage adoption blocker.
- **mutagen-2uh** (P2, bug) — Nested-module BEAM lookup uses non-qualified
  module name. Atlas reproduction. Common Phoenix/Ecto pattern.
- **mutagen-z9k** (P2, bug) — Literal mutator: pre-validate AST shape
  (Range step, capture args). v1 + builder reproductions.
- **mutagen-csv** (P3, task) — Default --timeout-ms too tight for
  app-shaped projects. Atlas reproduction (Ecto changeset mutations).

## Reports

- Cross-project synthesis (TL;DR of all findings):
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/mutagen_ex/2026-05-18-cross-project-synthesis.md
- v1 study (mutagen_ex self-dogfood):
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/mutagen_ex/2026-05-17-efficacy-study.md
- Atlas study:
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/atlas/2026-05-18-efficacy-study.md
- Builder study:
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/builder/2026-05-18-efficacy-study.md
- Engage (study-not-feasible note):
  ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/reports/engage/2026-05-18-efficacy-study.md

## Acceptance for the epic

- All four children closed.
- Re-running the atlas study no longer needs the `Mix.Tasks.MutagenApp`
  shim — stock `mix mutagen` works on Phoenix/Ecto projects.
- Re-running the builder study brings the 6 baseline-aborted candidates
  back to a usable state.
- mutagen_ex's existing self-test sandbox (the Lab.* approach from v1)
  continues to work (regression check for 1jx's opt-out flag).

## Out of scope for this epic

- HEEx / LiveView template mutator support (different category — net-new
  mutators, not bug fixes).
- Mutating files >900 LOC (the v2 studies didn't try anything bigger).
- Equivalent-mutant detection (still a v2.0 concern; v1 + v2 noted but
  did not file).

title: [EPIC] Phoenix/Ecto adoption polish — efficacy-study findings
